Requirement Statement: Establish a standardized, enterprise role-based access governance model that defines roles, maps roles to entitlements, and governs access decisions consistently across applications
Outcome: Consistent role-based access decisions across the enterprise
Rationale: Foundation requirement; all RBAC capabilities roll up here
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN a role-based access model is defined, WHEN access decisions are made across applications, THEN all decisions follow standardized role definitions with 100% consistency

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL enforce least privilege access by ensuring users receive only the access required for their job function and by explicitly identifying and governing privileged or sensitive access
Rationale: Drives privileged designation, SoD, and risk-based controls
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN least privilege policy is enforced, WHEN users are provisioned access, THEN access is limited to job function requirements AND privileged access is explicitly flagged

Requirement Statement: Govern the full access lifecycle, including request, approval, activation, review, revocation, and exception handling, in a repeatable and auditable manner
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN the access lifecycle is governed, WHEN any access event occurs (request/approval/activation/review/revocation/exception), THEN the event is logged with full audit trail
Move Types: Role Change, Leave of Absence, Expedited Termination

Requirement Statement: Produce complete, attributable, and auditable evidence of access decisions sufficient to support SOX and other regulatory audits without manual reconstruction
Rationale: Eliminates spreadsheets, screenshots, and tribal knowledge
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN SOX audit requirements, WHEN evidence is requested, THEN complete attributable evidence is retrievable without manual reconstruction within 24 hours

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL support scalable onboarding of applications, identities, and roles without bespoke redesign or manual controls
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN new applications/identities/roles need onboarding, WHEN onboarding is initiated, THEN standard templates are used with no bespoke development required

Requirement Statement: Define access intent, policy, and governance while delegating execution, automation, and enforcement to an enterprise IGA platform
Outcome: Clear separation of governance and execution
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN RBAC defines policy and IGA executes, WHEN access changes occur, THEN RBAC governs intent AND IGA automates enforcement with clear separation

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL provide a standardized discovery model to capture application RBAC readiness inputs
Rationale: Enables repeatable intake without bespoke analysis
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN a new application, WHEN discovery is initiated, THEN standardized intake questionnaire is presented with all required fields

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL require completion of minimum discovery criteria before entitlement mapping
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN discovery is incomplete, WHEN user attempts entitlement mapping, THEN system blocks with clear error message

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL require identification of business and technical owners
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN discovery submission, WHEN owner fields are empty, THEN system rejects with validation error

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL require documented role constructs or confirmation none exist
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN discovery process, WHEN role constructs exist, THEN they are documented; WHEN none exist, THEN explicit confirmation is required

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL require a current permission inventory
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN discovery process, WHEN permission inventory is missing, THEN system blocks progression

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL identify privileged access paths during discovery
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN discovery process, WHEN privileged access paths exist, THEN they are flagged for heightened governance

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL capture segregation-of-duties considerations
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN discovery process, WHEN SoD conflicts are identified, THEN they are captured and linked to compliance rules

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL track discovery lifecycle status
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN discovery lifecycle, WHEN status changes, THEN transitions are tracked with timestamps

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL retain discovery artifacts as audit evidence
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN audit request, WHEN discovery artifacts are requested, THEN all documents are retrievable

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL support RBAC governance for non-human identities
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN non-human identity, WHEN discovery is executed, THEN governance model applies equally to service accounts

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL support explicit definition of business roles
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN role creation, WHEN business role is defined, THEN name, description, owner, and eligibility are captured

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL enforce role eligibility criteria
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN role assignment, WHEN eligibility criteria not met, THEN assignment is blocked

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL require documented role purpose and owner
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN role creation, WHEN purpose or owner is missing, THEN system rejects creation

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL support role lifecycle states
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN role lifecycle, WHEN status changes (Draft/Active/Deprecated/Retired), THEN transitions are logged with approvals

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL ensure users inherit access only through roles
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN access model, WHEN user access is evaluated, THEN all access is inherited through roles only

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL automatically revoke access when role removed
Test Case ID: RBAC-TC-023, RBAC-TC-148, RBAC-TC-149, RBAC-TC-152, RBAC-TC-153, RBAC-TC-172
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN role removal, WHEN role is removed from user, THEN all associated access is automatically revoked within SLA
Move Types: Role Change, Leave of Absence, Expedited Termination

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL support role composition
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN role design, WHEN composite role is created, THEN child permissions are inherited and documented

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL support periodic role review
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN periodic review cycle, WHEN review is triggered, THEN all roles in scope are reviewed with outcomes recorded

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL require approval and justification for material role changes
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN role change, WHEN material change is requested, THEN approval and justification are required before implementation

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL enforce role-to-permission mapping
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN role definition, WHEN permissions are mapped, THEN role-to-permission relationships are enforced consistently

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL require justification for permissions in roles
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN permission assignment, WHEN permission is added to role, THEN business justification is required

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL flag privileged entitlements during mapping
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN entitlement mapping, WHEN privileged entitlement is identified, THEN privileged flag is automatically applied

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL require role owner approval for permission changes
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN permission change, WHEN permissions are modified, THEN role owner approval is required

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL apply privileged designation using governance criteria
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN privileged designation, WHEN governance criteria are met, THEN privileged flag is applied automatically

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL support role-based access requests
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN access request, WHEN user requests access, THEN role-based selection is presented (not individual entitlements)

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL route approvals based on governance rules
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN access request, WHEN approval is needed, THEN routing follows governance rules (manager, role owner, etc.)

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL record approval decisions as evidence
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN approval decision, WHEN approver acts, THEN decision is recorded with identity, timestamp, and justification

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL prevent fulfillment without approvals
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN incomplete approvals, WHEN fulfillment is attempted, THEN system blocks until all approvals complete

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL support time-bound / JIT role activation
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN JIT request, WHEN approved, THEN access is granted with explicit time boundary

Requirement Statement: Automatically revoke JIT access
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN JIT access, WHEN time boundary expires, THEN access is automatically revoked

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL record JIT events as audit evidence
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN JIT lifecycle, WHEN audit is requested, THEN full activation/deactivation evidence is available

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL support periodic access certifications
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN certification schedule, WHEN campaign launches, THEN all in-scope access is included

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL assign certifiers by governance rules
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN certification campaign, WHEN certifiers are assigned, THEN assignment follows governance rules with escalation

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL record certification decisions
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN certification review, WHEN certifier decides, THEN decision is recorded with timestamp and identity

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL trigger actions from certification outcomes
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN certification outcome, WHEN revoke decision is made, THEN access removal is triggered automatically

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL set certification cadence by risk tier
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN risk tier, WHEN certification cadence is configured, THEN frequency matches risk level (higher risk = more frequent)

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL support documented access exceptions
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN access exception, WHEN exception is requested, THEN justification, approver, and expiration are documented

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL enforce expiration or revalidation of exceptions
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN exception expiration, WHEN date is reached, THEN access is revoked OR revalidation workflow triggers

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL support emergency (break glass) access with review
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN emergency, WHEN break-glass access is invoked, THEN post-access review is automatically scheduled

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL retain exception and emergency evidence
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN audit request, WHEN exception/emergency evidence is requested, THEN complete trail is retrievable

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL trigger role review from repeated exceptions
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN repeated exceptions (3+), WHEN pattern is detected, THEN role review workflow is triggered

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL report who has access and why
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN access inquiry, WHEN report is generated, THEN user, role, entitlements, and justification are displayed

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL retain historical access records
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN historical inquiry, WHEN point-in-time access is requested, THEN snapshot shows access state with effective dates

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL support on-demand audit evidence
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN SOX audit, WHEN evidence package is requested, THEN complete package is generated without manual assembly

Requirement Statement: The system SHALL ensure evidence is complete and attributable
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN evidence review, WHEN records are examined, THEN timestamp, actor, action, and outcome are present

Requirement Statement: RBAC depends on IGA for execution
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN IGA outage, WHEN IGA is offline, THEN RBAC policy definitions remain accessible

Requirement Statement: IGA is system of record for roles and assignments
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN RBAC policy update, WHEN policy is published, THEN IGA correctly interprets and executes

Requirement Statement: IGA executes provisioning, certification, JIT
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN IGA execution, WHEN provisioning/certification completes, THEN evidence is returned to RBAC audit trail

Requirement Statement: IGA retains audit evidence for all access events
Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN IGA migration, WHEN platform is replaced, THEN RBAC definitions are portable via export/import

Acceptance Criteria: GIVEN hybrid environment, WHEN provisioning occurs, THEN both SaaS and on-prem targets receive consistent enforcement
Move Types: Role Change, Leave of Absence, Expedited Termination
